ety: inf. n. ii of ادب 'to discipline,' &c.
def: s.f. Discipline, correction, amendment, admonition, chastisement; erudition
subs: (-kī), To instruct, to teach good manners or politeness; to discipline, correct, admonish, chastise.

ety: for A. تعدية taʻdiyat, fem., inf. n. ii of عدو
def: s.m. Making (a verb) transitive; a transitive or causal verb.

ety: inf. n ii of درج 'to go step by step'
def: s.f. Gradation, scale
adj. Like that, such like; like him or it, &c.

ety: inf. n. ii of درس 'to read'
def: s.f. Instruction by means of reading; lecturing.
adv. = tad-api, q.v.s.v. tad.

ety: S. तात+इल+कः
def: adj. (f. -ī), Pertaining to, or related to, or descended from a father's elder brother
subs: s.m. Father's elder brother's son, cousin
s.f. Father's elder brother's daughter, cousin.

ety: see taft; tafta
def: s.m.f. Vapour, exhalation, steam, mist.

ety: inf. n. ii of فہم 'to understand'
def: s.m. Understanding; conceiving, apprehending.

ety: inf. n. v of فحص 'to search (for)'
def: s.m. Search, investigation, examination, scrutiny, inquiry, disquisition.

ety: inf. n. v of فجر 'to open (a channel for water, &c.)'
def: s.m. Bursting forth or out; suppuration.

ety: inf. n. v of فخر 'to glory or boast'
def: s.m. Glorying, boasting; pride, arrogance.

ety: inf. n. vi of فخر 'to glory or boast'
def: s.m. Glorying, boasting; boast, vaunt.

ety: inf. n. v of فکه 'to be free from straightness'
def: s.m. Enjoyment (of a thing); wondering (at), admiring; repenting, grieving, lamenting; penitence.

ety: inf. n. v of فقد 'to be lost'
def: s.m. Searching for anything lost; inquiring diligently, wishing to know; (fig.) seeking to gain the heart (of), sympathy, kindness, commiseration (syn. dil-joʼī, mihrbānī).

ety: inf. n. v of فکر 'to think (upon)'
def: s.m. Thinking (upon), considering; reflection, meditation, cogitation; anxiety.

ety: inf. n. v of فنّ; see fan
def: s.m. Diversion, relaxation, pastime, amusement, fun.
s.m. pl. (of tafriqa), Separations, divisions; intervals; instalments.

ety: inf. n. v of فرح 'to be glad'
def: s.m. Ease; leisure; refreshment. (The word is not classical Arabic.)

ety: inf. n. v of فرج 'to make an opening or break (in)'
def: s.m. Diversion, amusement, relaxation recreation (particularly by viewing natural objects, or by sight-seeing)
subs: s.f. Place of recreation (as a garden, or a theatre, &c.).

ety: inf. n. v of فرس 'to be skilled' (orig. 'in horsemanship,' and thence, in anything)
def: s.m. Considering or examining (a thing) closely; perceiving (a thing) by means of indications, evidences, &c.; judging from physiognomy; — intuitive perception (of); discernment, understanding, intelligence.

ety: inf. n. vi of فرق 'to make a difference (between)'
def: s.m. Separation, division.

ety: inf. n. vi of فال; formed from fāl, q.v.
def: s.m. Auguring; augury, &c. ( = tafawwul, q.v.).

ety: inf. n. vi of فوت 'to pass away or beyond'
def: s.m. Being far apart, being widely separated; distance; interval; difference, distinction; dissimilarity; discordance; disparity; adj. Widely separated, far away, distant, remote, absent; — adv. Apart; at a distance
subs: v.n. To make incongruous or discordant statements, to prevaricate
(-ko), To put, set, or cast apart or aside.

ety: inf. n. v of فوق 'to be superior (to)'
def: s.m. Exalting oneself (above others), pretending to a superiority (over others); superiority, ascendancy; — doing (anything) leisurely or by degrees.

ety: inf. n. v of فأل; but formed from fāl, q.v.
def: s.m. Auguring good (by or from), regarding as a good omen; taking an omen (from); presaging happily; augury

ety: inf. n. v of فضل 'to exceed, to excel'
def: s.m. Excess; excelling; deserving; conferring a benefit (on); beneficence, kindness, favour.

ety: act. part. of طفح 'to be full so as to overflow'
def: part. adj. Full to overflowing (a vessel, river, &c.), running over; full of wine, intoxicated.

ety: inf. n. ii of فہم
def: s.f. Making (one) understand or know; teaching instructing, informing.

ety: for A. تعفية, inf. n. ii of عفو 'to become effaced'
def: s.f. Effacing, obliteration; amending, adjusting.
s.m. Pride; affected magnificence.

ety: inf. n. ii of فرد 'to be or become single or alone'
def: s.f. Separation from others, retirement from the world (esp. for the sake of devotion); the being unique in science, &c.

ety: inf. n. ii of فرح 'to be glad'
def: s.f. Rejoicing, gladdening, exhilarating; gratification; diversion, amusement, recreation; fun, jest
subs: s.f. Diversion of mind; ease of mind; cheerfulness, hilarity
v.n. To divert or amuse oneself, to take recreation; to make fun, to be cheerful, to laugh and joke.

ety: acc. of tafrīḥ
def: adv. By way of diversion or amusement, for the sake of recreation; in fun, in jest.

ety: inf. n. ii of فرق; see tafriqa
def: s.f. Separating, dividing; distinguishing, discriminating; separation, division; analysis; distribution, partition, participation; distinction, difference, variety; classification; class, department; (in Arith.) subtraction; — jealousy, suspicion; misunderstanding
subs: (-kī), To separate; to analyse; to divide, partition, allot; to distinguish, discriminate, specify, define; to classify; to subtract
s.m. A deed specifying the shares allotted to different parties.

ety: for A. تفرقة, tafriqat, fem., inf. n. ii of فرق 'to make a difference (between)'
def: s.m. Difference, distinction; separation, division; variance, discord, disunion (syn. phūṭ); scattering, dispersion; distress; divorce
subs: (-meṅ), To sow discord (between), to set at variance, to separate
(-kā), To separate, divide; to distinguish, discriminate; to scatter, disperse.

ety: inf. n. ii of فرس 'to break the neck (of an animal)'
def: s.f. Breaking or tearing in pieces, rending.

ety: perf. part. of tafs-īdan; rt. Zend tafś, for tap = S. तप्
def: part. adj. Warmed, heated, burning; burnt; cracked or chapped (lips).

ety: inf. n. ii of فصل 'to separate or divide'
def: s.f. Analysis, separation, division; explaining distinctly or in detail; explanation; particulars, details
subs: (-kī), tafṣīl likhnā (-kī), To give the detail (of), exhibit in detail, to detail; to specify, particularize, explain in full or minutely

ety: acc. of tafṣīl
def: adv. In detail, &c. = tafṣīl-wār, q.v.s.v. tafṣīl.

ety: inf. n. ii of فسر 'to disclose; to explain'
def: s.f. Explaining, expounding, interpreting; explanation, exposition, interpretation (esp. of the Qorʼān); paraphrase; commentary.

ety: contrac. of tafta, q.v.
def: adj. & s.m. Hot, warm; — heat, warmth.

ety: perf. part. of tāftan; Pehl. tāpta; Zend tafta, rt. tap; S. tāpita
def: s.m. lit. 'Twisted; woven; heated,' &c.; a kind of silk, taffeta; a glossy cream colour (as in pigeons and horses).

ety: Zend tafta, rt. tap; S. तप्त, rt. तप्
def: part. adj. Heated; melted; hot, burning; displeased, grieved; twisted; — s.m. Quicklime
subs: adj. Displeased or grieved at heart.

ety: abst. s. fr. next
def: s.f. Heated state; heat, warmth; ardour, fervour; trouble, anguish, teen.

ety: inf. n. ii of فتش 'to examine,' &c.
def: s.f. Examination, scrutiny, investigation; diligent inquiry or search, research
subs: (-kī), To look closely (into), to search diligently (after), &c.

ety: inf. n. ii of فوض not in use
def: s.f. Committing (to), consigning (to); consignment (of); confiding (to), placing in trust or deposit; handing over (to); forwarding; cession; recommending; — giving (a woman) in marriage without a dowry
subs: v.t. To commit (to), consign (to), confide (to), hand over (to), place in trust or deposit, &c.

ety: inf. n. ii of فضح 'to disgrace'
def: s.f. Exposing the vices or faults (of); putting to shame; disgrace, disrepute, ignominy.

ety: inf. n. ii of فضل 'to exceed,' &c.
def: s.f. Excellence, pre-eminence
subs: An adjective in the comparative or superlative degree

def: s.m. The poplar; the elm; the tamarisk; a pomegranate; an ensign-staff.

ety: Zend taka, rt. tak = S. तक
def: s.f. Running, flying, rushing
subs: tag-ā-pūʼī, tag-ā-dau, tag-tāz, tag-dau, tag-o-pū, tag-o-tāz, or tag-o-dau, s.f. Running about in quest of employment or on business; great exertion or effort; close pursuit or search (after); bustle, toil, fatigue; anxiety, care, concern (syn. dauṛ-dhūp; udheṛ-bun).

ety: S. तन्तु+अग्र+कः; or तन्त्र+कः
def: s.m. Thread, cord (also written dhāgā)
subs: (-meṅ), To put stitches (in), to stitch (anything padded, as a quilt, &c.)
(-kā), To look closely (into or after), to scrutinize; to attend (to), entertain (a guest, &c.); to question closely; to call to account.

def: s.f. = tak, 'a scale.'
s.m. A poll-tax.

ety: S. तन्तु+अग्र+कः; or तन्त्र+कः
def: s.m. Thread, cord (also written dhāgā)
subs: (-meṅ), To put stitches (in), to stitch (anything padded, as a quilt, &c.)
(-kā), To look closely (into or after), to scrutinize; to attend (to), entertain (a guest, &c.); to question closely; to call to account.

ety: inf. n. vi of غبن 'to deceive, cheat'
def: s.m. Mutual cheating or deception, mutual endeavour to overreach.

ety: inf. n. vi of غفل 'to be unmindful,' &c.
def: s.m. Unmindfulness, heedlessness, forgetfulness, neglect, negligence, inattention, inadvertence, indifference, listlessness (syn. g̠aflat)
subs: (-meṅ), To be unmindful, or neglectful, &c. (in respect of)

def: s.f. = tag̠āful.

ety: tagā˚, base of tagānā+āʼī = Prk. एअव्वअइआ = S. (इ)तव्य+ता+इका(तिका)
def: s.f. The act of quilting; the price paid for quilting, &c.

ety: inf. n. v of غير; see tag̠īr
def: s.m. Becoming altered or changed; change, alteration; deterioration; removal; becoming vitiated or corrupt; falsification (of documents)

ety: inf. n. v of غلب 'to take or obtain by force'
def: s.m. Taking advantage (of), cheating; imposition; forgery; falsification; embezzlement; breach of trust; — adulteration
subs: v.n. To practise imposition (on), to commit forgery; to embezzle, &c.

def: s.f. = tag̠allub.
s.m. A combination of six short syllables.

ety: ta+gaṇa
def: s.m. (in Pros.), The measure called hypo-bacchic or anti-bacchic.

ety: caus. of tagnā
def: v.t. To cause to be quilted; to get stiched together.

ety: inf. n. v of غني 'to be free from want'; 'to be content'
def: s.f. Contentment; singing; cooing; celebrating (a mistress) in verse.
s.f. See s.v. tag for compounds of which tag is the first member.

ety: prob. from A. غار
def: s.m. Platter, pail, bucket, tub, trough; a mason's mud or lime pit
subs: v.n. To make mortar, or slake lime, in a pit.

def: s.m. The shrub Tabernœmontana coronaria, and a fragrant powder prepared from it.

ety: Prk. टगरो; S. टङ्ग+रः
def: s.m. Borax; — wanton play or sport; wandering of the mind, confusion, bewilderment, perplexity.

ety: cf. tagṛī
def: s.m. A plate or leaf of metal; a medal; a device on a shield (cf. tamg̠a).
s.m. Hail, a hail-storm.
s.f. A small tub or trough, a kneading trough.
v.n. = ṭagharnā, q.v.

ety: inf. n. vi of غير; see g̠air
def: s.m. Difference, diversity, discrepancy.

ety: caus. from tag = S. tak
def: v.t. To run after, to chase, pursue closely; to assault (cf. rageṛnā).
s.m. The husband of a wet-nurse (syn. dhāʼoṛā).

ety: see tāgā
def: s.m. A strand or thread of a twist; sewing thread
subs: (-par), To tie a thread (on the right wrist, in honour of Lakshmī).

ety: dim. of taggā, or tāgā, q.v.
def: s.f. A strand or thread of a twist; a kind of fishing line (not used with a rod, prop. ćau-taggī, q.v.).
v.n. = ṭagharnā, q.v.

ety: ṭaghar˚ = S. त्रख्, or त्रङ्ग्+र
def: v.n. To move or walk to and fro ( = ṭahalnā), to roam, stroll; to roll; to flow; (cf. ḍag; ḍagar; ḍagarnā; ṭahal and ṭahalnā).
v.t. = ṭaghrānā, q.v.

ety: caus. of ṭagharnā
def: v.t. To cause to move to and fro; to rub (with oil, &c.); to cause to roll, to roll; to cause to flow.
s.f. = tagṛī, q.v.

ety: for A. طاغ t̤āg̠iṉ, act. part. of طغي 'to exceed the just limit'
def: adj. & s.m. Rebellious, refractory, violent; — a rebel, an insurgent.

ety: contrac. of A. تغيير tag̠yīr, inf. n. ii of غير; formed from g̠air, 'other'
def: s.f. Changing, altering (for the worse); change, alteration; removing (from office), discharging, dismissing; discharge, dismissal; — adj. Changed, altered; discharged, dismissed
subs: (with acc. of direct, and abl. of indirect object), To change, alter; to remove, dismiss (a person, from an office)
v.n. To be changed or altered, to alter; to be removed (from office), be dismissed.

ety: for A. tag̠yīrāt, pl. of tag̠yīr
def: s.f. Changes, alterations, &c.
s.f. The state of alteration or change; revolution; discharge, dismissal, deposition, disgrace.

ety: from tāgā, q.v.
def: v.t. To stitch a quilt, to quilt ( = tāgā ḍālnā).

ety: from P. tak or tag = S. tak
def: v.t. To cause to run or gallop.
adj. Changed, converted; transposed, &c.
s.m. = tamg̠a, q.v.

ety: from tāg
def: v.t. To thread (a needle).

ety: from tāgā, q.v.
def: v.t. To quilt; to stitch together.

ety: tag˚ = tak˚ = S. स्तक?+ṛā = Prk. अडओ = S. र+कः
def: adj. Stout, strong, robust.

ety: S. टगर+कः
def: adj. (f. -ī), Squint-eyed.

ety: caus. of ṭagarnā
def: v.t. = ṭaghrānā, q.v.

ety: tāga, q.v.+ṛī = Prk. डिआ = S. र+इका
def: s.f. The thread which Hindūs (esp. children) fasten round the waist.

ety: tāg, q.v.+ṛī = Prk. अडिआ = S. र+इका
def: s.f. A thread, or a gold or silver chain, worn round the waist; a girdle, a zone (syn. kardhanī).

ety: inf. n. ii of غرب 'to go away,' &c.
def: s.f. Removing to a distance; putting away; banishment, transportation: imprisonment.
s.f. = tagāʼī, q.v.
s.f. See tag̠īr.

ety: for A. تغذية tag̠ẕiyat, fem., inf. n. ii of غذو 'to feed'
def: s.m. Food, nourishment ( = g̠iẕā).

ety: S. धा?
def: s.f. Plait, fold, ply ( = tah); multiplicity, perplexity.

s.f. Ground; site; floor; surface; bottom, underneath; foundation; depth; layer, stratum; fold, plait, ply; — real meaning or intent; hidden meaning; depth of meaning, profundity, subtleness; allusion, insinuation ( = ramz; kināya); — adj. (in comp.) Under, beneath, underground
subs: s.m. The ground or site of a market
s.f. Ground-rent of a stall in a market; ground-rent or tax paid by shop-keepers in a market-place
adj. & adv. One upon or over another; fold within fold; complicated, involved; (in Bot.) plicate; — plait by plait, fold by fold; every fold
v.t. To pile or arrange one upon or over another
s.m. A cloth worn round the waist and passing between the legs
s.f. Drawers worn by women under the sāṛī
s.m. A cap or cloth worn under the turban (syn. bat̤āna)
(-kī), To lick the bottom (of a platter, &c.) clean, to lick (a platter, &c.) clean
(-kī), To plait, fold, place fold on fold; to drink glass after glass (of)
s.m. A room under ground, a subterranean abode; a lower story; cellar, vault; a cavern

adj. Having bottom or foundation; having depth (lit. & fig.), deep, profound, subtle (e.g. yěh shěʻr tah-dār hai); rational; — tah-dārī, s.f. The having bottom or foundation; deepness, depth, profundity, &c.
s.f. The scrapings of a pot, the burnt part of victuals that sticks to the bottom of a pot (syn. khurćan)
adj. (f. -ī), Deep, profound, hidden, occult, mysterious; involved
v.t. To fold; to arrange; to set aside, put by, put up; to dispose of, settle, decide
v.t. To fold up; to put carefully by; to keep (a thing) to ooneself
(-kī), To get to the bottom (of), to get to the root (of), to discover
s.m. A disease affecting the flanks (of a horse, arising from excessive thirst)
s.m. The mounting at the lower end of a scabbard or sheath
s.m. An agreement; a treaty (of peace, &c.); a bill of articles or items agreed upon by two parties
adj. Inlaid (iron with gold, or a sword-hilt with gold and precious stones)
v.n. To sink to the bottom; to settle; to subside; to sink into the mind
adv. Upside down, topsy-turvy; — tah-o-bālā karnā, v.t. To turn upside down; to subvert, to overthrow.

ety: pl. of تحفه tǒḥfa
def: s.m. Rarities, curiosities, choice articles; presents (com. used in conjunction with the sing., e.g. tǒḥfa-taḥāʼif).

ety: inf. n. v of حير 'to be dazzled by'
def: s.m. Being astonished, confounded, or disturbed; astonishment, amazement, wonder.

ety: inf. n. v of هجو 'to join (letters), to spell'
def: s.f. Spelling; orthography
subs: s.m. The letters of the alphabet.

ety: inf. n. v of هجد 'to sleep'; 'to wake, watch,' &c.
def: s.m. Sleeping soundly; sleeplessness, wakefulness; a form of prayer repeated during the night.
s.f. Shooting pain, throb, &c. = ṭasak, q.v.

ety: inf. n. v of حق 'to be just, right, &c.'
def: s.m. Proving to be truth, reality, or fact; ascertainment, certain knowledge, certainty (cf. taḥqīq).

ety: inf. n. v of حکم 'to restrain,' &c.
def: s.m. Commanding, ruling; control, power, authority, dominion (see ḥukūmat); usurping authority, usurpation; deciding judicially, passing sentence, condemning
subs: (-par), To exercise control or authority (over), to command, rule, &c.

def: v.n. = ṭabhakhnā and ṭasaknā, q.v.

ety: S. त्रख्+र; cf. ṭagharnā; ṭaghalnā
def: s.f. = ṭahal
subs: or ṭahal-ṭakor karnā (-kī), To serve, attend (on); to labour, drudge

ety: inf. n. ii of حلحل 'to remove'
def: s.m. Removing (from a place), quitting; becoming in motion.

ety: see ṭahal
def: v.n. To walk to and fro, or up and down; to take a walk, to rove, ramble, saunter; to move off or away, to be off; to depart this life, to die
subs: v.n. To move away, to be off; to die.

ety: from ṭahalnā
def: s.f. A woman-servant, a maid-servant ( = ṭahlūʼī); a house-wife.

ety: inf. n. vi of حلف
def: s.m. Conspiring together, leaguing; swearing (both the plaintiff and the defendant).
conj.; see s.v. tā.

ety: inf. n. v of حمل 'to bear'
def: s.m. Enduring patiently; patience, endurance, long-suffering, resignation, forbearance; meekness, humility; truce, peace
subs: (-meṇ), To be patient or forbearing (under, or in), to bear patiently, endure, forbear.

def: adv. (Old H.) = tahāṅ, q.v.

ety: S. तत्+स्थाने
def: adv. (correl. to jahāṅ, q.v.), There, thither; then, at that juncture
subs: adv. There and there, in each place.

def: adv. contrac. of tahāṅ, q.v.

ety: from tah
def: v.t. To fold, ply, wrap, &c. ( = tah karnā, q.v.s.v. tah).
adv. (prov.) = tahāṅ, q.v.
adv. (prov.) = tahāṅ, q.v.

ety: for A. طہارة, inf. n. of طہر 'to become clean, or pure'
def: s.f. Purity, cleanliness; sanctity; ceremonial purification; ablution, the act of performing the ablution termed g̠usl, and that termed wuẓū; and that termed istinjā
subs: To perform the ablution termed g̠usl, &c.; to wash; to purify oneself (see ābdast karnā).

def: v.n. = ṭahalnā, q.v.

ety: inf. n. v of حرك 'to move'
def: s.m. Becoming movent (as a letter); motion, movement; agitation.

ety: inf. n. vi of حشي
def: s.m.f. Standing apart; excepting (against a thing, by saying ḥāshaʼllāh 'God forbid'); care, concern, fear (e.g. be-taḥāshā, adv. 'fearlessly').

ety: prob. from S. नश, tahas being an apa-śabd
def: adj. & s.m. Destroyed, ruined; scattered, dispersed; overthrown, subverted; topsy-turvy, confused; — destruction, ruin; overthrow, subversion; dispersion; confusion.

ety: inf. n. v of حسر 'to grieve; regret'
def: s.m. Grieving (for); regretting; grief, regret ( = ḥasrat); condolence.

ety: inf. n. v of هتك 'to tear (a veil, &c.); to expose'
def: s.m. Exposure, disgrace, infamy; defamation (cf. hatk).
adv. (Braj) = tau-bhī, q.v.s.v. tau.

ety: inf. n. vi of هون 'to be light, or vile'
def: s.m. Contempt; neglect, negligence, carelessness, sloth.

ety: inf. n. v of هوع 'to vomit,' &c.
def: s.m. Inclination to vomit, sickness at stomach.

ety: inf. n. v of حور 'to return'
def: s.m. Haste, despatch, celerity; anger. (N.B. The word is not classical Arabic.)

ety: inf. n. v of هور 'to impel,' &c.
def: s.m. Plunging inconsiderately (into any business); rashness, temerity, intrepidity, impetuosity, ardour, fury; — oppression; — adj. Intrepid, brave, &c.

ety: inf. n. ii of هدّ 'to break,' &c.
def: s.f. Terrifying; threatening; terror, alarm; threat, menace.

ety: contract. of tahāṅ-hīṅ
def: adv. There, in that same place, exactly there; thither, in that very direction.

ety: act. part. of طہر 'to be clean,' &c.
def: adj. Clean, pure, unsullied, chaste.
s.f. A dish, rice boiled with baṛī, q.v.

ety: prob. fr. t̤āhir
def: s.f. A dish of rice and adaurī, q.v.; rice cooked with turmeric.

ety: for A. تہية tahīyat, fem., inf. n. ii of هيأ 'to be prepared'
def: s.m. Preparation, provision; putting in order, arrangement
s.f. pl. (of next, q.v.), Salutations, &c.

ety: for A. تحية; inf. n. ii of حيّ 'to live'
def: s.f. Salutation, greeting, compliment, congratulation; prayer, benediction.
s.m. pl. (of tahīya, q.v.), Preparatiens, arrangements.

ety: inf. n. ii of حق 'to be just, right, &c.'
def: s.f. Ascertaining, verifying the truth (of); ascertainment, inquiry, investigation, trial, verification; exactness, precision; truth, fact, certainty; — adj. Carefully ascertained or verified, well-established; authentic, true, actual, real, indubitable, sure, certain; — adv. For a fact, truly, indeed, certainly
subs: (-kī), To make inquiry or investigation; to inquire (into), to ascertain, to investigate; to verify; to make sure (of).

def: adv. According to truth or fact; certainly.
s.f. (pl. of taḥqīq, but com. used as a sing. in Urdū), Inquiries; inquiry, investigation; verification
subs: taḥqīqāt-ě-mā-qabal, s.f. Preliminary inquiry or investigation
(-kī), To inquire, &c. = taḥqīq karnā, q.v.s.v. taḥqīq.

def: adj. Sure, certain, confirmatory.

ety: inf. n. ii of حکم
def: s.f. Putting in authority; authority; arbitration.

ety: inf. n. ii of حقر 'to be contemptible'
def: s.f. Rendering contemptible; despising; contempt, scorn, disdain; neglect.

ety: caus. of ṭahalnā
def: v.t. To cause to walk to and fro or up and down, to lead about (as a horse or a child); to make (one) dance attendance; to get (one) out of the way, to put or turn aside, to remove, to dismiss; to put off, postpone; to prevaricate ( = ṭālnā).

ety: inf. n. ii of حلف 'to swear'
def: s.f. Making (one) swear, exacting an oath.

ety: inf. n. ii of حلّ 'to untie; to loose'
def: s.f. Untying; loosing; dissolving; discussing (in a medical sense); solubility; solution; digestion, concoction; assimilation; — making lawful
subs: v.t. To dissolve, to discuss; to digest; to assimilate; — taḥlīl-karne-wālā, adj. (in Med.) Dissolvent, discutient
v.n. To be dissolved; to waste away; to be digested.

ety: inf. n. ii of هلّ 'to call aloud, to proclaim,' &c.
def: s.f. Praising God, acknowledging the true God (by saying lā ilāha illaʼllāh, 'There is no god but God').
s.f. (poet.) = ṭahal; — s.m. (local) = ṭahlūʼā.

ety: ṭahal, q.v.+Prk. इउओ = S. (इ)तृ+कः
def: s.m. A manager of household concerns; a servant, attendant, drudge, menial, labourer.
s.f. = ṭahlūʼā; ṭahalnī; qq.v.

ety: for A. تہلکة tahlukat, fem., inf. n. of هلك
def: s.m. Anything ending in destruction; ruin, perdition; consternation, dismay, alarm, panic; pang, agony.

ety: inf. n. ii of حمد 'to praise'
def: s.f. Praising (God) much or repeatedly (saying alhamdǒ liʼllāh).

ety: inf. n. ii of حمل 'to bear'
def: s.f. Making (one) bear or carry (a burden, &c.), imposing a burden (on), burdening, loading; importuning; asking more than one's right.
s.m. A thick branch, large bough (of a tree).

ety: dim. of ṭahnā
def: s.f. A small branch, a spray, twig.

ety: for A. تہنئة, inf. n. ii of هنأ 'to be joyful,' &c.
def: s.f. Wishing (one) joy or prosperity, congratulating; congratulation.

ety: see ṭhok-nā
def: s.m. Blow, thump, slap, cuff; push, shove; jog, nudge; goad, spur.
v.t. To beat, thump, hammer ( = ṭhoknā); to push, shove; to impel, urge on, goad, spur.

ety: onomat.
def: s.m. The cry of a new-born child, or of a babe in arms.

ety: inf. n. ii of حرف 'to alter in form,' &c.
def: s.f. Altering (words from their proper meanings); alteration; inversion, transposition (of a word or letter); forming an anagram; an anagram; mistranscription clerical error; deliberate alteration of a word so as t, change its meaning, falsification; — cutting the nib of a pen obliquely.
s.f. pl. (of taḥrīf), Alterations, changes; anagrams.

ety: inf. n. ii of حرك 'to move'
def: s.f. Putting in motion; motion, movement, agitation, commotion; incitement, stimulus, encouragement, excitement, instigation, temptation
subs: taḥrīk karnā (-kī), To move, incite (to), to urge, actuate, excite, encourage, tempt; to affect, touch.

ety: for A. تحريمة taḥrīmat, fem.; from taḥrīm, inf. n. ii of حرم 'to be prohibited,' &c.
def: s.m. The saying allāhǒ akbar, "God is great,' at the commencement of prayer; the commencement of prayer (when the person praying is prohibited, by uttering the takbīr, from saying and doing anything extraneous to prayer); — assumption of a pilgrim's garb (since this entitles the person to reverence or respect).

ety: inf. n. ii of حر 'to be hot'; 'to be free'
def: s.f. Setting at liberty, manumission; — writing elegantly and accurately; writing, description; a written statement or declaration, a document; fee for writing (anything for another); ornamental lines (on a drawing or picture), lines; pencilling, painting (e.g. surme-kī taḥrīr); — adj. Written, dated
subs: tahrīr-uqlaidas, (or simply) taḥrīr, s.f. Euclid's elements
s.f. Writing between lines; interlineation: taḥrīr-ě-z̤ahrī, s.f. A writing on the back, endorsement
(-kī), To write, to describe, to record

ety: acc. of taḥrīr
def: adv. In writing; by writing.
s.f. pl. (of taḥrīr), Writings, &c.
adj. Written, in writing (e.g. taḥrīrī s̤ubūt, 'written proof').

ety: inf. n. ii of حرص 'to desire eagerly,' &c.
def: s.f. Making greedy; inflaming, inciting; instigation, stimulation
subs: v.t. To inflame, stimulate, to entice, allure. (N.B. The form in classical Arabic is taḥrīẓ, not taḥrīṣ.)

ety: inf. n. ii of حصل 'to be produced; to be realized'
def: s.f. Getting, acquiring; collecting; gain, acquisition, profit, attainment; learning; collection (particularly of revenues or rents); the revenue jurisdiction of a taḥṣīl-dār; the station or court of a taḥṣīl-dār
subs: s.f. The office, duty, or jurisdiction of a sub-collector; collectorship
(-kī), To get, gain, acquire; to attain, learn; to collect (revenue, &c.).

def: s.f. pl. (of taḥṣīl), Acquisitions; collections.

ety: from taḥsīl
def: v.t. To acquire; to collect, gather (a tax = taḥṣīl karnā, q.v.s.v. taḥṣīl); to oppress, to afflict.

ety: inf. n. ii of حسن 'to possess beauty,' &c.
def: s.f. Rendering good, comely, or pleasing; regarding as good or beautiful, approving; approbation; applause, acclamation, cheers
subs: (-kī), To approve; to commend, praise, applaud.

ety: A. naḥs+taḥs, a jingling or insignificant word
def: adj. Spoilt; broken, scattered, dispersed; overthrown, destroyed, ruined, unfortunate (cf. tittar-bittar).

ety: in A. phrases taḥtu
def: s.m. The location which is beneath, the interior part, the under part, the inferior part, depth; the nadir; — subjection, dominion, possession, authority, control; — prep. & adv. Beneath, under; in subjection (to), under the control (of, -ke)
subs: s.m. The depth of the moist earth; the under part of the earth, the nether regions
s.m. Change of the moon, conjunction with the sun
taḥt-taṣarruf, s.m. & adv. Subjection, dominion, authority, control; — under the dominion (of, -ke), &c.
s.m. & adv. Jurisdiction, control, &c. = taḥt-ě-taṣarruf, q.v.
adj. & adv. Literal, verbal (as a translation); — literally, verbally
adv. Under, under control or authority (of, -ke), in subjection (to); below, at the foot (of a page, &c.)
(-ke), To come under the authority or control (of)
v.t. To keep under, maintain in subjection, to control; to have or keep in (one's) possession, to possess, hold, have
v.t. To bring under, to reduce to subjection, to conquer, subdue; to bring under control or authority; to take possession of.

ety: fr. S. दृह् 'to grow'; cf. ḍahḍahā
def: s.m. Flourishing state; freshness; bloom, beauty, splendour; conspicuousness.

ety: fr. S. दृह् 'to grow'; cf. ḍahḍahā
def: s.m. Flourishing state; freshness; bloom, beauty, splendour; conspicuousness.
adj. Lower, inferior; small, short (applied to letters or diacritical points); placed beneath, pointed below.
pron. (Braj) = us-se, 'of or from him.'

ety: inf. n. of طہر
def: s.m. A thing (such as water) with which one cleanses or purifies; a means of purifying or cleansing; — adj. Cleansing, purifying; clean, pure
subs: 'A purifying, or a pure, draught,' the water of Paradise, nectar.

def: adv. (prov.) = tahāṅ, q.v.
adv. (prov.) = tahāṅ, q.v.

ety: inf. n. ii of حول 'to be changed, &c.
def: s.f. Altering, changing, transmuting; change, transfer; renovation; return; (in Astron.) passing of the sun, moon, or a planet from one sign to another; care, trust, charge; a deposit, revenue credit; cash, funds, capital; a treasury; — adv. In the charge (of, -ke)
subs: s.f. Misappropriation of a trust, embezzlement
s.m. Cash-keeper, cashier, treasurer (esp. in a provincial treasury)

def: s.f. pl. (of taḥwīl), Alterations, changes; revolutions, &c.

ety: inf. n. ii of هذب 'to prune; to clean,' &c.
def: s.f. Purifying; adjusting, adorning; correcting, amending; correction, amendment, edification, refinement, polish
subs: adj. Refined, polished, polite, educated, civilized.

ety: inf. n. ii of حذر 'to be cautious,' &c.
def: s.f. Cautioning, putting on one's guard; causing to fear, threatening.

ety: Prk. तविआ; S. तप+इका; cf. tayā; tawā
def: s.f. A kind of baking-pan of iron, a frying-pan; a griddle.

ety: S. तावत्
def: adj. (Braj), So many ( = titne)
subs: adv. So many times, so often.

ety: inf. n. of طوي 'to fold or fold up, to roll up,' &c.
def: s.m. Folding, folding up, rolling up; a fold, crease; a plait
subs: v.t. To fold, to roll up; to pass or cross over, to traverse; to bring to a conclusion, to wind up, to close, to dispose of; to constrain; to abridge

ety: see tawā
def: s.f. An earthen frying-pan.

ety: fem. of tāyā = tāʼū
def: s.f. The wife of a father's elder brother; paternal uncle's wife; a term of respectful compellation or mention of a female generally.

ety: for A. تائبة
def: adj. Repenting of sin, repentant, penitent.

ety: for A. تائبة
def: adj. Repenting of sin, repentant, penitent.

ety: inf. n. ii of ايد 'to become strong'
def: s.f. Strengthening; corroboration, confirmation; assisting; assistance, help, aid, support; a written voucher in support (of a claim)
subs: (-kī), To strengthen, corroborate, confirm; to assist, aid, support
s.m. An assistant clerk, a private assistant.

ety: pl. of tāʼīd
def: s.m. Helps, aids, &c.

ety: act. part. of طوف 'to go or walk round'
def: part. adj. & s.m. Going round, making the circuit (of), circumambulating; — patrol, night-watch; — a vision in a dream; a visitation from the devil; — name of a city in Hijāz.

ety: for A. طائفة, fr. t̤āʼif, q.v.
def: s.m. (orig.) 'Nocturnal rounds or patrols'; a people, tribe, troop, company, body, band, gang; a company of dancing girls and musicians; — a caravan; — suit, equipage.
s.m. A small partridge; — adj. (in Pers.) Empty.

ety: Prk. तेल्लं; S. तैलं
def: s.m. Expressed oil (prepared from sesamum, mustard-seed, &c.); storax, gum, benzoin, incense
subs: s.f., tail-pāyī, s.m., tail-pak, s.m. lit. 'Oil-drinker' ; a cockroach; any oil-drinking beetle
s.m. Marriage presents, consisting of oil, cocoanuts, &c., sent by the bridegroom to the bride shortly before his own arrival to celebrate the marriage
adj. As black as an oiled baking-pan; glossy black
(-par), To anoint the head, shoulders, and hands and feet of the bride and bridegroom with oil mixed with turmeric during the marriage ceremony; (hence) the marriage ceremony above described
s.m. Oil-cake a cake made of oily seed
(-meṅ), To apply oil (to), to rub oil (on), to oil, anoint
v.n. To show the (his or her) face in a cup of oil imbedded in pulse (to a convalescent person, or to one returned from a journey)
s.f. A wick, the cotton of a lamp
(-kā), To express or to extract oil (from)
(-kā), Oil to be expressed or extracted (from); oil or grease to exude (from); to sweat profusely (through heat, toil, &c.).

def: s.m. The country along the coast south of Orissa as far as Madras, the modern Carnatic.
s.m. = tilangā, q.v.

ety: Prk. तेल्लिओ; S. तैलिकः
def: adj. & s.m. Relating or belonging to oil, oily, &c.; — a caste whose business it is to make or to sell oil; a member of that caste; an oil manufacturer, oil-grinder, oilman; — an unclean or dirty fellow
subs: s.m. A class of mendicants who beg for oil which they apply to their heads and clothes; — a dirty vagabond
s.m. An oilman's ox; (fig.) a hard-working wretch.

def: s.m. Oil manufacturer, oilman ( = telī).

ety: S. तैलिनी
def: s.f. a woman of the tailī caste; a woman who prepares oil; the wife of a telī or oilman.
s.f. = tailin or telin, q.v.; — a wick, the cotton of a lamp ( = tail-mālī); — a kind of insect found in oil, a species of fly (used in medicine instead of the Spanish fly; — cf. talīnī).
s.f. = tāʼī; — postpn. = taʼīṅ; qq.v.

ety: Prk. थाणि or थाणे; S. स्थाने
def: postpn. (governing gen. with -ke), To, up to: (-ke taʼīṅ = ko; e.g. uske taʼiṅ = usko; apne taʼīṅ, = āp-ko or apne-āp-ko).

ety: act. part. of طعن 'to pierce, stab' (with a spear, or with the tongue
def: s.m. A reviler, an asperser, a slanderer, calumniator.

ety: corr. of taʻaiyun, or of taʻyīn; but in the latter case it should be fem.
def: s.m. Appointing; appointment; deputation (see next)

ety: act. part. of طير 'to fly'
def: part. adj. & s.m. Flying; — a bird, a winged creature
subs: s.m. 'The bird of the lote-tree,' a name of the angel Gabriel.

ety: inf. n. of طير 'to fly'
def: s.m. Flying; — a flying thing, a winged creature, a bird, &c. (syn. t̤āʼir).

ety: rt. of tairnā, q.v.+āk; Prk. आवको = S. आपि+अकः(आपकः)
def: s.m. A swimmer ( = pairāk, q.v.).

ety: tairāk+ī = Prk. इआ = S. इका
def: s.f. The art of swimming ( = pairākī).

ety: tair˚ or tīr˚ = Prk. तर(इ) or तीर(इ) = S. तर(ति) or तिर(ति), rt. तॄ
def: v.n. To swim; to float (syn. pairnā); — v.t. To swim over or across; to go through, become familiar with, to master (a business, or a subject, &c.)
subs: (-meṅ), To be well versed (in), be familiar (with).

def: s.m. A he-goat; a buck; a stag.
adj. & adv., contrac. of next, q.v.

ety: Prk. तइसओ; S.ताट्टश+कः
def: adj. (correl. to jaisā), That-like, such as that, such; — adv. In that manner, in like manner, so; at that moment
subs: adv. In like manner, thus, so.

ety: Prk. तइसिल्लो; S. ताट्टश+इलः
def: A dialectic form of taisā, q.v.
adv. See s.v. taisā.

ety: inf. n. of طيش 'to be light, or inconstant,' &c.
def: s.m. Levity, folly; anger, passion, rage, choler, indignation
subs: or t̤aish-meṅ ānā, v.n. To become enraged, &c.

ety: corr. of تعويذ taʻwīẕ?
def: s.m. A charm, amulet, a particular ornament (worn round the neck or arm) viewed generally as an amulet.

ety: prob. onomat.; cf. S. ṭiṭṭibhī
def: s.f. A sandpiper; a pewet (syn. t̤aṭīhrī).

ety: see titil
def: s.m. One of the astronomical periods called karans; — deity.
adj. & s.m. Produced from partridges; — a partridge ( = tītar); a flock or a covey of partridges.
adj. Relating to the Tittiri branch of the Yajur Veda (as a student, a teacher; a section, a text, &c.).

ety: see next
def: s.m. A follower of the Tittiri branch of the Yajur Veda.

ety: intens. n. fr. طير 'to fly'
def: adj. Flying, running swiftly, fleet, spirited (horse); — s.m. A winged animal.
adj. = P طياري t̤aiyārī, s.f. See تيار taiyār, and تياري taiyārī.

ety: for A. طيار t̤aiyār, 'sharp, quick'
def: adj. Ready, alert, willing; prepared, ready-made, finished, completed, complete; fully developed, plump, fat (as an animal, &c.); in full vigour, arrived at puberty, robust; ripened, ripe (fruit)
subs: v.t. To make or get ready, to prepare; to provide, arrange, put in order; to train; to fatten
v.n. To be ready, be prepared; to get ready, be on the alert, &c.

def: s.f. The state of being prepared; preparedness, readiness; preparation, getting ready, making; arrangement; show, pomp, splendour, magnificence; plumpness, fatness: robustness
subs: (-kī), To make preparation (for), to prepare, get ready (for), &c.

def: s.f. = P طيار t̤aiyār, adj. See تيار taiyār, and تياري taiyārī.

ety: v.n. fr. طيب; see t̤īb
def: adj. Good, pleasant, agreeable, sweet.

ety: S. त्वच्
def: s.f. The bay-tree, Laurus cassia, woody cassia; cassia bark, cinnamon.

ety: see tajnā
def: adj. & s.f. Abandoning, forsaking, giving up.

ety: A. also tāj, fr. the P.; old P. taka; Armen. thag
def: s.m. A crown, diadem, tiara; a high-crowned cap; crest, tuft, plume; comb (of a bird); — a suit in cards
subs: s.m. A bestower of crowns, an emperor, a king
s.m. The comb of a cock; the cock's-comb flower, Amaranthus, or Celosia cristata
adj. & s.m. Crowned, diademed; crested; — a crowned head, king, sovereign
adj. & s.m. Wearing a crown, crowned; — a king, prince

ety: inf. n. v of جدّ 'to cut off'
def: s.m. = A تجديد tajdīd [inf. n. ii of جدّ], s.f. Renewing; renewal, novelty.

ety: inf. n. vi of جہل 'to be ignorant'
def: s.m. Feigning ignorance; pretended ignorance (of), connivance; apathy, indifference
subs: v.n. To feign or affect ignorance.

ety: inf. n. ii of جلجل 'to move about'
def: s.m. Being moved; agitation; commotion; sinking into the ground, becoming depressed (as a foundation, &c.).

ety: inf. n. v of جلو 'to become clear,' &c.
def: s.f. Manifestation; clearness, lustre, brightness, brilliancy, splendour, glory
subs: adj. Rendering bright, splendid, or glorious.

def: s.f. pl. Manifestations; lustres, illuminations; beatific visions.

ety: inf. n. v of جمل 'to be beautiful,' &c.
def: s.m. Dignity; pomp, parade; splendour, magnificence: retinue; furniture; conveniences (see next).
s.m. pl. Household furniture, movables; conveniences, articles of luxury.

ety: inf. n. v of جنب 'to put at a distance'
def: s.m. Retiring, withdrawing (from), avoiding, shunning, refraining or desisting (from)
subs: (-se), To hold aloof (from), to retire, withdraw (from), to refrain (from), &c.

ety: inf. n. v of جرد 'to strip, denude'
def: s.m. Stripping or denuding oneself; cutting oneself off from society, living in solitude; solitude; celibacy.

ety: inf. n. v of جسّ 'to search for'
def: s.m. Searching carefully, examining, investigating, exploring, curiously prying (into), spying; search, inquiry, investigation; curiosity, inquisitiveness
subs: v.n. To search diligently, to explore; to pry (into), to play the spy, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَجَسُّس (tajassus), from Arabic تَجَسُّس (tajassus).

ety: inf. n. vi of جسر 'to be daring'
def: s.m. Carrying it high and insolently; boldness, firmness, daring, intrepidity, temerity.

ety: inf. n. vi of جوز 'to pass through or beyond'
def: s.m. Passing (beyond), surpassing; passing (over), overlooking, forgiving; departing (from), deviating, straying; exceeding (one's authority, &c.), transgressing (just bounds or limits); deviation; diversity; excess, transgression; outrage, insolence
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَجَاوُز (tajāwuz), borrowed from Arabic تَجَاوُز (tajāwuz), from تَجَاوَزَ (tajāwaza).

ety: corr. of A. tajazzǒ, inf. n. v of جزأ 'to divide'
def: s.f. Dividing, separating into small pieces; analysis.

ety: inf. n. ii of جدّ
def: s.f. = A تجدد tajaddud [inf. n. v of جدّ 'to cutoff'], s.m. Renewing; renewal, novelty.

ety: inf. n. ii of جہز 'to despatch, slay'
def: s.f. Fitting out, equipping; expediting, arranging, adorning; carrying to the grave, burying, burial
subs: (-kī), To fit out (an expedition, army, &c.), to equip, arrange, raise, &c.

ety: inf. n. ii of عجل 'to make haste,' &c.
def: s.f. Hastening; haste, despatch, expedition; speediness, agility.

ety: act. part. of تجر 'to traffic'
def: s.m. A merchant, trader.
(tad+jṅa), adj. & s.m. lit. 'Knowing that'; knowing, experienced (in), familiar (with); a knowing or intelligent man, a connoiseur.

ety: S. त्यजनीयं, rt. त्यज्
def: v.t. To abandon, give up, relinquish, quit, leave, forsake, desert
adj. = tattwa-jnā, q.v.s.v. tattwa.

ety: inf. n. ii of جنّس jannasa, a denom. from jins, q.v.
def: s.f. Making of the same kind, making homogeneous (with); resemblance, analogy; (in Phet.) alliteration; punning (especially to the eye); pun; equivoque
subs: s.f. Using words which are written with the same letters but with different vowel points, a kind of equivoque in writing (e.g. pal, pil, pul in the following verse of Saudā's
mauj-ě-ćashm-ě-ʻāshiqān de toṛ pal-meṅ pil-ke pul
'the waves from the eyes of lovers would in a moment demolish a bridge by their shock').

ety: for A. تجربة tajribat, fem.; inf. n. ii of جرب 'to have the mange'
def: s.m. Experiment, test, trial, proof, assay; experience; probation
subs: adj. Experienced, expert, versed (in), conversant (in), skilful; — tajriba-kārī, s.f. Experience, practical knowledge
(-kā), To make trial (of), to try, prove, assay; to experience.

ety: for A. تجربة
def: s.f. = next, q.v.

ety: inf. n. ii. of جرد; cf. tajarrud
def: s.f. Stripping, denuding, divesting (of); separating (a thing from another); separation, solitude; celibacy.
adj. & s.m. See s.v. ṭāj.

ety: inf. n. ii of جوف 'Being hollow'
def: s.f. Hollowing out, making concave.

ety: inf. n. ii of جوز 'to pass through,' &c.
def: s.f. Permitting, allowing; approving; permission; approbation; inquiring into, examining, considering; inquiry, investigation, consideration, deliberation; view, opinion, judgment; estimate; resolution, determination; trial; decision, judgment, sentence; contrivance, scheme, plan, device
subs: tajwīz-ě-jadīd, s.f. Another or fresh trial, re-trial; revision; review of a decision or judgment
adj. Requiring to be determined or decided; for adjudication or decision, to be tried
(-kī), To inquire (into), to examine, investigate; to determine, decide; to pass sentence, pronounce judgment; to choose, elect; to prescribe, appoint; to contrive, devise, plan
v.n. To be mquired (into), be investigated, &c.

def: adj. Tried, proved, determined.

ety: S. द्राक्षा
def: s.f. A vine; creeper; branch of any tree growing like a vine; grapes.

ety: S. दघ्न; or perhaps a contrac. of tā+lag; cf. talak
def: postpn. To, toward, up to, near to, even to, as far as, so much as, to the extent (of), including, with; — till, until, as long as, while, during, for.
s.f. Look, glance; aim, &c. ( = tāk, q.v.)
subs: tak lagānā (-par) = tāk bāṅdhnā, &c., q.v.s.v. tāk.

ety: Prk. तक्का; S. तर्का
def: s.f. A balance, a scale (esp. a large one for weighing heavy goods).
s.f. Sight, look, gaze, stare, &c. ( = tak and tāk, q.v.); temper, nature, disposition (in these senses probably akin to S. sthā)
subs: adv. With fixed or intent look, with steadfast gaze
and ṭak lagānā, see s.v. tak or tāk.

ety: Ar. form of old P. تاك tāk; and of Pehl. tāk = P. tāh or tā, q.v.
def: s.m. An arched building; an arch; a cupola, vault; a recess (in a wall), a niche; a shelf; a projecting part, a cornice, a ledge, &c.; a window; a balcony; — adj. & s.m. Single, sole; uneven, odd (opp. to juft); singular, rare, unique, unmatched, unequalled, unrivalled; — a fold, plait, ply
subs: (-kā), To place a lighted lamp as a votive offering in a niche (in a mosque, &c.)
v.n. 'To settle on a ledge,' &c.; to become an accomplished fact; to be brought home (to); to be confirmed (as a report, e.g. urṭī urṭī t̤āq baiṭhī, 'the report is confirmed')
(-ko), To lay on the shelf; to put or set aside; to neglect, abandon; to refuse; to disobey
v.n. To be laid on the shelf, to be set or thrown aside; to be rendered ineffective or useless
s.m. The game of odd or even.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian طاق (tâq), ultimately from Middle Persian [script needed] (tʾg /⁠tāg⁠/, “arch”).

ety: v.n. fr. tāknā, q.v.
def: s.f. Look, regard, glance, gaze; fixed look, stare; peep, watch, look-out, expectation; view, aim, the act of aiming (at); pointing (of a dog, &c.)
subs: s.f. Mutual gazing or staring, exchange of glances; bo-peep
s.f. Precise moment, nick of time
(-par), To fix the gaze (on), look fixedly (at); to take aim (at); to point (as a dog, &c.); to look longingly (at), to covet
(-kī), To come in expectation (of), to come with an eye (to)
tāk-meṅ rahnā (-kī), To be on the look out or watch (for), to lie in wait (for).

ety: Prk. टक्कओ; S. टङ्ककः
def: s.m. A ready, pert, or plain answer; a point-blank refusal
subs: s.f. A sole individual life without relations or dependants

ety: for A. طاقة, n. of un. fr. t̤āq, q.v.
def: s.m. A single piece; a piece (of woollen cloth, &c.).

ety: inf. n. v. of کبر 'to be great'
def: s.m. Pride, haughtiness, arrogance, loftiness, presumption, grandeur.
s.f. = takabbur, q.v-

ety: inf. n. v of قيض 'to take'
def: s.m. Mutual surrender, silent exchange of an article and its cost.

ety: inf. n. vi of قبل 'to come forward, to approach,' &c.
def: s.m. Encountering, standing face to face, the being drawn up in mutual opposition.

ety: 3rd pers. sing. perf. act. of conj. v. of قدس 'to be pure or holy'
def: adj. Sanctified, pure, holy (said of God).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَقَدُّس (taqaddus), borrowed from Arabic تَقَدُّس (taqaddus).

ety: inf. n. v of کدّ 'to labour'
def: s.m. Labour, toil; hardship. (Not classical Arabic.)

ety: inf. n. v of قدم 'to precede'
def: s.m. Preceding; priority, precedence, pre-eminence.

ety: inf. n. v of کدر 'to be turbid,' &c.
def: s.m. Being turbid; being disturbed or troubled (in mind, &c.); muddiness, turbidness; dregs; dulness; disturbed state (of mind), disquietude; sullenness, moroseness.

ety: inf. n. v of قدس
def: s.m. Being pure and holy; purity, sanctity, holiness.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَقَدُّس (taqaddus), borrowed from Arabic تَقَدُّس (taqaddus).

ety: inf. n. vi of قدم 'to precede,' &c.
def: s.m. Being ancient, antique or old; preceding, going before; (in law) such a distance of time as suffices to prevent punishment (similar to the 'statutory limitations' of English law).

ety: inf. n. v of کفل 'to be security (for)'
def: s.m. Taking security; becoming security for another; surety; bail.

ety: inf. n. vi of کہل 'to be slow,' &c.
def: s.m. Indolence, negligence, remissness.

ety: from ṭakā
def: s.f. A tax, duty, collection, imposition; a cheap trull ( = ṭake-wālī).

ety: S. टङ्क+Prk. आइत्तो = आवेंतो = S. आपयन्
def: adj. & s.m. Possessing ready money, moneyed, rich, wealthy
subs: ṭakait-rāʼe, s.m. A moneyed man (com. as a nickname).

ety: S. टङ्क+Prk. आइत्तो = आवेंतो = S. आपयन्
def: adj. & s.m. Possessing ready money, moneyed, rich, wealthy
subs: ṭakait-rāʼe, s.m. A moneyed man (com. as a nickname).

ety: inf. n. v of قيد 'to be bound'
def: s.f. (m.?), Application, attention, diligence, industry, assiduity, care; strictness; overseeing, superintending, oversight, superintendence, surveillance, observation, vigilance; enjoining, injunction
subs: (-par), To superintend, look closely (after); to impress (upon), enjoin, caution, &c.

def: s.f. pl. (of taqaiyud), Injunctions, cautions, &c.
s.f. pl. (of taklīf), Impositions, difficulties, distresses, hardships, trials.

ety: inf. n. v of قلب 'to turn; to invert,' &c.
def: s.m. Being turned, being inverted; turn, change, inversion, conversion, &c. (see the trans. form taqlīb).
